name: English
description: The global lingua franca, widely used in business and technology.

name: Spanish
description: A widely spoken Romance language, native to Spain and Latin America.

name: French
description: The language of diplomacy, spoken in France and many parts of Africa.

name: German
description: A major European language spoken primarily in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland.

name: Hindi
description: A major Indian language and one of the most spoken languages worldwide.

name: Marathi
description: An Indian language spoken predominantly in Maharashtra.

name: Tamil
description: A Dravidian language spoken mainly in Tamil Nadu and Sri Lanka.

name: Telugu
description: A South Indian language spoken widely in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana.

name: Bengali
description: The language of West Bengal and Bangladesh, rich in literature and culture.

name: Gujarati
description: A language of Gujarat, known for its business influence and literature.

name: Punjabi
description: The language of Punjab, widely spoken in India and Pakistan.

name: Malayalam
description: A Dravidian language spoken mainly in Kerala.

name: Kannada
description: A South Indian language spoken in Karnataka.

name: Odia
description: A classical Indian language spoken in Odisha.

name: Urdu
description: A poetic language spoken in India and Pakistan.

name: Japanese
description: The language of Japan, known for its three writing scripts.

name: Korean
description: The language of Korea, known for its unique Hangul script.

name: Chinese
description: A major world language, encompassing Mandarin and Cantonese dialects.

name: Russian
description: A Slavic language spoken across Russia and Eastern Europe.

name: Portuguese
description: A Romance language spoken in Portugal, Brazil, and parts of Africa.

name: Italian
description: The language of Italy, known for its musical and cultural influence.

name: Dutch
description: A Germanic language spoken in the Netherlands and Belgium.

name: Greek
description: The language of Greece, rich in history and philosophy.

name: Turkish
description: The language of Turkey, with roots in Central Asia.

name: Arabic
description: A major Semitic language spoken across the Middle East and North Africa.

name: Hebrew
description: A Semitic language and the official language of Israel.

name: Persian
description: A poetic language spoken in Iran, Afghanistan, and Tajikistan.

name: Thai
description: The tonal language of Thailand, written in a unique script.

name: Vietnamese
description: A tonal language spoken primarily in Vietnam.

name: Swedish
description: The Germanic language of Sweden, known for its melodic tones.

name: Danish
description: The language of Denmark, closely related to Swedish and Norwegian.

name: Norwegian
description: The language of Norway, with two written forms: Bokmål and Nynorsk.

name: Finnish
description: A unique Finno-Ugric language spoken in Finland.

name: Hungarian
description: A non-Indo-European language spoken in Hungary.

name: Polish
description: A Slavic language spoken in Poland, rich in consonants.

name: Czech
description: A Slavic language spoken in the Czech Republic.

name: Ukrainian
description: The official language of Ukraine, closely related to Russian.

name: Romanian
description: A Romance language spoken in Romania and Moldova.
